title: Hybrid Neural-MPM for Interactive Fluid Simulations in Real-Time
authors: Xu, Huang, Zou, Savva, Wei, Chen
problem_task: Real-time interactive fluid simulation by replacing the expensive part of MPM with a GNN and falling back to a numerical solver when needed.
method_summary: Hybrid system: a GNN-based neural physics module predicts particle dynamics at low spatio-temporal resolution; a fallback safeguard routes hard frames to a classical MPM solver; a diffusion-based controller generates external force fields for interactive control.
key_metrics: Latency, fidelity vs. MPM reference, interaction quality.
key_results: Real-time interactive fluid demos with low latency while preserving fidelity via the fallback safeguard.
limitations: Demonstrated on fluids only; cloth-specific anisotropy, contact, and thin-shell stress are not addressed; controller component is out of scope for our project.

title: A Moving Least Squares Material Point Method with Displacement Discontinuity and Two-Way Rigid Body Coupling
authors: Hu, Fang, Ge, Qu, Zhu, Pradhana, Jiang
problem_task: Modern, fast MPM variant (MLS-MPM) used by most GPU MPM codes including Taichi and Warp.
method_summary: Reformulates MPM via moving least squares so APIC/PolyPIC arise naturally from a Galerkin weak form; introduces a faster stress-divergence discretization (~2x speedup); adds Compatible PIC (CPIC) using colored distance fields for cutting and thin boundaries; supports two-way rigid coupling.
key_metrics: Wall-clock per step vs. APIC/PolyPIC; visual fidelity on cutting / thin-boundary scenes.
key_results: ~2x faster MPM step; clean handling of cutting, thin boundaries, rigid coupling.
limitations: Original implementation focuses on fluids/solids and 2D; cloth specifics (bending, anisotropy) not directly addressed.

title: Learning Mesh-Based Simulation with Graph Networks (MeshGraphNets)
problem_task: Learn forward dynamics on a mesh by passing messages on its graph; supports cloth among other systems.
method_summary: Encode-process-decode GNN with several rounds of message passing on the mesh; trained on one-step prediction with input noise; supports adaptive remeshing; demonstrated on FlagSimple, FlagDynamic, SphereDynamic, aerodynamics, structural mechanics.
key_metrics: Rollout RMSE, generalization across resolutions, qualitative cloth visualization.
key_results: Strong rollout stability across many systems including cloth (FlagDynamic); resolution-independent dynamics via adaptive remeshing.
limitations: Pure data-driven; no physics fallback; energy not conserved long-term; can drift on long rollouts without noise injection.

title: Large Steps in Cloth Simulation
problem_task: Stable cloth simulation at large time steps via implicit Euler on a mass-spring/triangle-mesh cloth.
method_summary: Cloth as triangular mesh with continuum-style stretch/shear/bend forces; implicit Euler yields a large sparse linear system at each step solved via modified conjugate gradient that simultaneously enforces per-particle constraints; unified damping treatment.
key_metrics: Stable step size, time-per-frame, qualitative drape and dynamics.
key_results: Step sizes orders of magnitude larger than explicit baselines; constraints met exactly regardless of CG iterations.
limitations: Mass-spring/cloth-only; no contact richness; no plasticity.

title: Learning to Simulate Complex Physics with Graph Networks (GNS)
authors: Sanchez-Gonzalez, Godwin, Pfaff, Ying, Leskovec, Battaglia
problem_task: General GNN simulator for particle-based physics: fluids, granular, deformable.
method_summary: Particles are graph nodes with k-NN edges; encode-process-decode GNN predicts per-particle accelerations; trained one-step with Gaussian noise on inputs to stabilize long rollouts.
key_metrics: One-step error; rollout MSE; generalization to 10x more particles and longer horizons.
key_results: Generalizes from 1k-particle training to ~10k particles and thousands of steps at test time on water/sand/goop.
limitations: No physics fallback; relies on input noise as the main regularizer; cloth-specific anisotropy and bending not addressed.

title: The Material Point Method for Simulating Continuum Materials (Course Notes)
problem_task: Canonical tutorial on MPM: P2G, grid update, G2P, constitutive models, explicit and implicit integration.
method_summary: 52-page course notes covering MPM theory, weak form, kernels, deformation gradient update, isotropic elasticity differentiation, explicit and implicit time integration, plasticity, production usage.

title: AnisoMPM: Animating Anisotropic Damage Mechanics
authors: Wolper, Chen, Li, Fang, Qu, Lu, Cheng, Jiang
problem_task: Anisotropic damage and fracture in MPM using continuum damage mechanics, applicable to fiber-aligned materials (cloth-relevant).
method_summary: Non-local CDM-based damage evolution combined with MPM; anisotropic hyperelasticity for transversely isotropic and orthotropic materials; phase-field-style damage; integrates with MLS-MPM-style transfers.
key_metrics: Visual fidelity of fracture; control of preferred fracture directions.
key_results: Tearing of meat, fruit, cheese; controllable anisotropic fracture aligned with material grain.
limitations: Fracture-focused, not real-time; cloth not the headline application; expensive damage solve.

title: Solver-in-the-Loop: Learning from Differentiable Physics to Interact with Iterative PDE Solvers
problem_task: Train a learned correction module while it interacts with the iterative numerical solver during training; reduce numerical error of low-resolution solvers.
method_summary: Embed the solver inside the training loop so the network sees realistic input distributions including its own past corrections; compare to non-interactive learning baselines on unsteady wake flow and forced advection-diffusion.
key_metrics: MAE / RMSE on velocity; long-rollout stability; correction quality.
key_results: Interactive solver-in-the-loop training significantly outperforms one-step / pre-trained baselines on long rollouts.
limitations: Demonstrated on Eulerian fluid PDEs; not directly on MPM cloth, but the principle of training-time fallback interaction informs our hybrid loop.

title: DiffTaichi: Differentiable Programming for Physical Simulation
authors: Hu, Anderson, Li, Sun, Carr, Ragan-Kelley, Durand
problem_task: Differentiable programming language for high-performance physical simulators; enables gradient-based learning and optimization of simulation parameters.
method_summary: Imperative DSL generating reverse-mode gradient kernels via source-code transformation; light tape recording program structure; 10 example simulators including elastic objects, MPM, smoke, billiards, robots.
key_metrics: Lines-of-code, runtime vs. CUDA / TF; gradient correctness.
key_results: Differentiable elastic simulator 4.2x shorter than CUDA, 188x faster than TF; gradient-based optimization of various physics tasks.
limitations: Taichi-specific (not Warp); we are not committing to differentiable MPM in this project, so used as background only.

title: A Material Point Method for Snow Simulation
problem_task: Clean, often-cited MPM case study with elasto-plasticity for snow.
method_summary: MPM with elasto-plastic constitutive model that captures snow's hardening and clumping behavior; demonstrates plasticity within the MPM transfer cycle.
key_metrics: Visual fidelity vs. real snow; production cases (Frozen).
key_results: High-fidelity snow simulation used in production at Disney.
limitations: Snow-specific constitutive model; cloth-specific anisotropy and thin-shell stress not addressed.

title: A Material Point Method for Thin Shells with Frictional Contact
authors: Guo, Han, Fu, Gast, Tamstorf, Teran
problem_task: Thin-shell (cloth) simulation in MPM with frictional contact and self-collision.
method_summary: FEM-discretized shell potential energy combined with MPM transfers; Kirchhoff-Love kinematics; bending/lamina potentials; works on garments, walking, twisted shirts with self-collisions.
key_metrics: Qualitative drape, walk cycle, twist; contact correctness.
key_results: High-quality cloth MPM with frictional self-contact on garments.
limitations: Implementation complexity is high; not real-time; treats bending energy via FEM rather than purely particle-based.

title: Physics-inspired Estimation of Optimal Cloth Mesh Resolution
authors: Zhang, Wang, Liu, Pei, Xu, Wang
problem_task: Pre-simulation method to estimate optimal nonuniform cloth mesh resolution from fabric properties using experimental wrinkle theories.
method_summary: Use characteristic wrinkle wavelength derived from experimental cloth-physics theories to assign nonuniform per-region mesh resolution; ensure smooth resolution transitions across garment-construction seams.
key_metrics: Compute time and memory; visual quality of wrinkles; comparison to uniform-resolution meshes.
key_results: Visually accurate garment results at significantly lower compute than uniform fine meshes.
limitations: Mesh-based, not MPM; resolution decisions are pre-simulation only; out-of-scope for our learned grid-update but useful as cloth-physics context.

title: A Physics-embedded Deep Learning Framework for Cloth Simulation
problem_task: Cloth simulation by a CNN that encodes mass-spring system with separate branches for linear, nonlinear, and time-derivative cloth physics.
method_summary: Convolutional network whose architecture mirrors the spatial structure of a mass-spring grid; three branches model linear, nonlinear, and time-derivative components; pluggable to traditional or sub-NN external forces and collisions.
key_metrics: Agreement with mass-spring baseline; predictive realism without retraining on new cases.
key_results: Reasonable agreement with baseline simulators across cloth animation cases without retraining.
limitations: Mass-spring rather than MPM; CNN-grid representation does not generalize to arbitrary topologies; weaker stability story than GNS / MGN.
